DP01.feature: Encapsulate storage/access logic for every new model.
DP01.likelyHackathonTask: Create DAO classes for Reaction, Community, Notification, Report, etc.
DP01.notes: Current backend already has DAO<T>, UserDAO, and PostDAO.

DP02.feature: Combine DAO, indexes, and persistence behind a richer interface.
DP02.likelyHackathonTask: Repository coordinates multiple DAOs and indexes.
DP02.notes: Useful when DAO alone becomes too low-level.

DP03.feature: Ensure single global instance for DAOs/managers.
DP03.likelyHackathonTask: Apply singleton to managers where duplicate storage would break correctness.
DP03.notes: Already visible in current UserDAO/PostDAO/DataManager style.

DP04.likelyHackathonTask: Factory maps event type to Notification object.

DP05.feature: Switch between AVL, BST, sorted array, or B-tree.
DP05.likelyHackathonTask: Factory creates implementation without changing DAO code.

DP06.feature: Switch between CSV, JSON, XML formatted data.
DP06.likelyHackathonTask: Create reader/writer families for the same format.

DP08.feature: Support exact, prefix, any-word, all-word search.

DP09.feature: Filter feed by blocks, community, muted topics, or deleted state.
DP09.likelyHackathonTask: Each filter is a strategy or specification.

DP10.feature: Compute report priority differently by policy.

DP11.feature: Notify subscribers when posts receive replies/likes/mentions.
DP11.likelyHackathonTask: Subject publishes events; observers create notifications.

DP12.feature: Update trending indexes when content is added.
DP12.likelyHackathonTask: Trending service listens to new post/message events.

DP13.feature: Keep visible message window synchronized with sorted data.
DP13.likelyHackathonTask: SortedDataSlice observes insertions and updates visible window.

DP14.likelyHackathonTask: Move permission checks into state objects.
DP14.notes: Current userstate package already suggests this.

DP16.feature: Report moves through Open, UnderReview, Resolved, Dismissed.
DP16.likelyHackathonTask: State controls valid transitions and operations.

DP17.feature: Traverse feed without exposing storage implementation.
DP17.notes: Iterator is already used in DAO and PostDAO.

DP18.feature: Decorate or wrap an iterator to skip hidden items.
DP18.likelyHackathonTask: Wrap an existing iterator and apply visibility predicate.

DP19.feature: Treat post thread and comment nodes uniformly as containers.
DP19.likelyHackathonTask: Composite nodes can contain child comments.

DP20.likelyHackathonTask: Command object can execute and undo.

DP21.likelyHackathonTask: Command allows undo/redo of text edits.

DP22.feature: Add markdown, mention highlight, or profanity filter.

DP23.feature: Wrap feed provider to add filters dynamically.
DP23.likelyHackathonTask: Decorators add blocked user, muted topic, and deleted filters.

DP24.feature: Expose simple API for UI/client code.
DP24.likelyHackathonTask: Hide DAOs, indexes, DataManager, and state transitions behind one class.
DP24.notes: Very likely for a backend mini project.

DP25.feature: Adapt old saved-row schemas to current models.
DP25.likelyHackathonTask: Adapter converts legacy data to current serializer format.

DP26.feature: Adapt JSON/XML objects to DataPipeline serializer type.
DP26.likelyHackathonTask: Adapter makes different document formats look like common rows.

DP27.feature: Build complex posts with optional fields safely.
DP27.notes: Good if Post gains many fields.

DP28.likelyHackathonTask: Template method handles common serialize/deserialize steps.

DP29.likelyHackathonTask: Proxy controls access and caches results.

DP30.feature: Avoid null checks for unauthenticated or missing entities.
DP30.likelyHackathonTask: Return safe null-object instead of Java null where appropriate.
DP30.notes: Use carefully: course tests may expect null in existing APIs.

DP31.likelyHackathonTask: Each handler either passes, flags, or rejects.

DP32.feature: Capture previous object state for restore.
DP32.likelyHackathonTask: Save snapshots of post/message text before mutation.

DP33.feature: Run analytics over posts/messages/comments without changing model classes.
DP33.likelyHackathonTask: Visitor calculates word count, mention count, or score summary.

DP34.notes: Less likely but useful for many notifications.

DP35.feature: Clone template users/posts for generated demo data.
DP35.likelyHackathonTask: Prototype creates test/demo objects from templates.

DP36.feature: Services depend on interfaces, not concrete DAOs.

DP37.feature: Separate domain objects from storage implementation.
DP37.likelyHackathonTask: Abstraction is DataStore; implementation is CSV/JSON/XML.
DP37.notes: Similar to Abstract Factory but service-facing.

DP38.feature: Represent validation rules as reusable objects.
DP38.notes: Good for replacing hard-coded if statements.

DP39.feature: Every command execution emits an audit event.
DP39.likelyHackathonTask: Combine Command and Observer for audit logging.

DP40.feature: Single service coordinates state, DAO, indexes, and persistence.
DP40.likelyHackathonTask: Facade checks permissions and delegates work.
possibleApi: reply(User actor, UUID postId, String text)
DP40.notes: Most practical design-pattern task for hackathon.

DS01.feature: Search posts by words in topic/title/body without scanning every post.
DS01.likelyHackathonTask: Build an index that maps each normalized token to the posts containing it.
DS01.coreStructure: HashMap<String, Set<UUID>, HashSet, optional SortedData<Post> for ordered results
DS01.notes: Very likely because current Post has a topic but no search feature.

DS02.likelyHackathonTask: Index message content and allow global or per-thread search.

DS03.feature: Find posts/messages tagged with hashtags such as #java or #comp2100.
DS03.likelyHackathonTask: Extract hashtags and map each tag to matching posts/messages.
DS03.coreStructure: Map<String, Set<UUID>, Map<String, Integer>, PriorityQueue for trending tags
DS03.notes: Often paired with persistent hashtag snapshots.

DS04.likelyHackathonTask: Map mentioned username or userId to all mentioning content.

DS05.feature: Show all posts written by one user on a profile page.
DS05.likelyHackathonTask: Maintain a mapping from userId to the user’s posts.

DS06.feature: Show all comments/messages by one user.
DS06.notes: Can be rebuilt from all posts/messages.

DS07.feature: Get newest, oldest, and range of posts by creation time.
DS07.likelyHackathonTask: Use balanced sorted storage keyed by timestamp and postId.
DS07.notes: Very likely because SortedData/AVL already exist.

DS08.feature: Keep messages in chronological order for a thread.
DS08.likelyHackathonTask: Use SortedData<Message> to store messages by timestamp/thread/poster/id.

DS09.feature: Maintain posts sorted by votes/replies/activity score.
DS09.likelyHackathonTask: Remove and reinsert when a score changes to preserve ordering.

DS10.feature: Return top N active posts efficiently.
DS10.likelyHackathonTask: Use a priority queue for quick top retrieval; handle score updates carefully.
DS10.notes: If Java PriorityQueue is used, score update often means remove + reinsert.

DS11.feature: Rank users by reputation or karma.
DS11.likelyHackathonTask: Map users to scores and maintain a sorted leaderboard.

DS12.likelyHackathonTask: Prevent duplicate reaction by same user and support reaction type changes.
possibleApi: react(UUID userId, UUID targetId, ReactionType type)
DS12.notes: This is a classic HashMap/HashSet task.

DS13.likelyHackathonTask: Keep one vote per user per target and compute score efficiently.

DS14.likelyHackathonTask: Map each user to a set of saved post ids.

DS15.feature: Hide content from blocked or muted users.
DS15.likelyHackathonTask: Map blocker to blocked users and muted topics.

DS17.feature: Suggest users based on follow graph.
DS17.likelyHackathonTask: Compute candidates followed by people I follow.
DS17.coreStructure: Graph traversal, Set intersection, PriorityQueue for ranking

DS18.feature: Track which users belong to each community.
DS18.likelyHackathonTask: Map community id/name to member ids and roles.

DS19.feature: Store posts inside communities sorted by time/score.
DS19.likelyHackathonTask: Map community to sorted post collection.

DS21.feature: Prioritize critical notifications over normal ones.
DS21.notes: Useful for admin warnings or mentions.

DS22.feature: Process reports by severity and age.
DS22.likelyHackathonTask: Priority queue of report objects with stable comparator.
DS22.notes: Moderation is a common AdminState extension.

DS23.feature: Undo delete/ban/lock actions in reverse order.

DS24.feature: Store previous versions for reverting edits.

DS26.feature: Flatten nested comments in thread order.
DS26.notes: Iterator design pattern and stack usage.

DS28.likelyHackathonTask: Maintain a visible window over sorted messages.
DS28.notes: Directly matches the current SortedDataSlice idea.

DS29.likelyHackathonTask: Deque of recent post ids with capacity.

DS30.feature: Group private messages by user pair/conversation.
DS30.likelyHackathonTask: Map conversation key to sorted messages.
possibleApi: send(UUID sender, UUID receiver, String text)

DS31.likelyHackathonTask: Queue unread messages per user or conversation.

DS32.feature: Prevent spam by limiting actions per time window.
DS32.likelyHackathonTask: Queue timestamps per user; remove expired timestamps.

DS34.feature: Suggest usernames, communities, or keywords by prefix.

DS35.feature: Suggest hashtags by prefix and popularity.
DS35.likelyHackathonTask: Trie nodes with terminal frequency count.

DS36.feature: Find most frequent words in posts/messages.
DS36.likelyHackathonTask: HashMap counts plus heap for top K.

DS37.feature: Merge several sorted feeds into one global feed.

DS38.feature: Connect posts with shared tags, links, or cross-references.

DS39.feature: Find connected discussions through related-post graph.

DS41.feature: Map login tokens to users with expiry.
DS41.likelyHackathonTask: HashMap token -> session and priority queue by expiry.

DS42.feature: Index attachments by file type, owner, or post.
DS42.likelyHackathonTask: Maps from owner/post/type to attachment ids.

DS43.feature: Maintain pinned posts for a community or admin.
DS43.likelyHackathonTask: Set of pinned post ids plus ordered list.

DS44.likelyHackathonTask: Map user to draft ids, sorted by update time.
DS44.notes: Pairs with persistent data and post State.

DS45.feature: Dynamically discover Message objects from arbitrary runtime object graphs.
DS45.likelyHackathonTask: Build a recursive traversal engine capable of searching unknown nested structures containing Message objects.
DS45.coreStructure: Reflection traversal, recursive graph walking, Iterator traversal, cycle detection, runtime inspection.
DS45.notes: Suitable for framework-style infrastructure tasks, runtime inspection systems, serializers, object explorers, debuggers, or generic traversal engines.

DS46.feature: Search many posts by id with disk-friendly structure.
DS46.notes: Likely if B-tree/refactoring lecture is assessed.

DS47.feature: Find posts/messages in a time interval.
DS47.likelyHackathonTask: AVL range traversal or TreeMap sub-map idea.
DS47.notes: Useful for recent activity and moderation audits.

DS48.feature: Connect topics that appear together in posts.

PD01.likelyHackathonTask: Create a serializer and data pipeline for reaction records.

PD02.likelyHackathonTask: Persist one vote per user per target.
PD02.notes: Derived scores can be rebuilt after load.

PD04.notes: Works with AdminState and report queue.

PD09.feature: Save user block and topic mute lists.
PD09.likelyHackathonTask: Persist block relationships and muted topics.

PD10.likelyHackathonTask: Persist direct messages independent of posts.

PD11.notes: Needed if direct messages are feature-rich.

PD14.likelyHackathonTask: Persist who did what and when.
PD14.notes: Useful for audit log and Command undo.

PD15.feature: Save theme, notifications, and privacy settings.
possibleApi: setSetting(UUID user, String key, String value)

PD16.feature: Save derived keyword index to avoid rebuild cost.
PD16.likelyHackathonTask: Persist keyword-target rows or explicitly choose rebuild-on-load.
PD16.notes: A good discussion point: derived data may not need persistence.

PD18.likelyHackathonTask: Persist scores or rebuild from votes.
PD18.notes: Scores may be derived from votes.

PD19.feature: Save sessions if remember-login is required.
PD19.likelyHackathonTask: Persist token, user id, expiry time.
PD19.notes: May be unnecessary if sessions are transient.

PD20.feature: Add version/schema information to saved files.
PD20.likelyHackathonTask: Read version first, then select deserializer or migration path.

PD21.feature: Load old saved files into new object model.
PD21.likelyHackathonTask: Implement a migration from v1 post schema to v2 with extra fields.
PD21.coreStructure: input v1: id,poster,topic; output v2 adds createdAt,score,community

PD22.feature: Export all application data into one backup.
PD22.likelyHackathonTask: Write a full snapshot of users, posts, messages, and new feature data.
PD22.coreStructure: snapshot.json or multiple csv files in backup folder
PD22.notes: Can be CSV or JSON depending on allowed tools.

PD23.feature: Avoid corrupted saves if writing fails.
PD23.likelyHackathonTask: Write to temporary file, then rename on success.

PD24.feature: Add JSON format support behind FormattedFactory.
PD24.likelyHackathonTask: Implement reader/writer compatible with existing DataPipeline abstraction.
PD24.notes: Likely because persistent data lectures include JSON.

PD25.likelyHackathonTask: Implement XML reader/writer or export-only view.

PD26.feature: Improve CSV handling for commas, quotes, and newlines.
PD26.notes: Very likely because messages contain arbitrary text.

PD27.feature: Reconnect messages, reactions, and communities after loading.
PD27.likelyHackathonTask: Load raw objects first, then validate references.

PD28.feature: Ensure entities load in dependency order.
PD28.likelyHackathonTask: Users before posts; posts before messages; relationships after entities.
PD28.notes: Current DataManager can be extended here.

PD29.feature: Define what happens if saved file is missing.
PD29.likelyHackathonTask:
Missing optional data file should not crash; missing required file may initialise empty
DAO.

PD30.likelyHackathonTask: Reject, skip, or collect errors for malformed saved data.
possibleApi: onMalformedRow(String file, int line, Exception e)

PD31.likelyHackathonTask: Check uniqueness, foreign keys, and invariants.

PD32.feature: Persist every action instead of only current state.
PD32.likelyHackathonTask: Record create post, reply, like, delete, login, etc.

PD33.feature: Persist deleted flag instead of removing records.
PD33.likelyHackathonTask: Keep deleted posts/messages with deletedAt and deletedBy.

PD34.feature: Move old posts/messages to archive files.
PD34.likelyHackathonTask: Write older data to archive while keeping current feed smaller.

PD36.feature: Load initial demo data for testing.

PD37.likelyHackathonTask: Track dirty state and batch write changed files.

PD38.likelyHackathonTask: Write errors to a text log.
PD38.notes: Logging is explicitly useful for persistent data.

PD39.feature: Generate human-readable schema docs from serializers.
PD39.likelyHackathonTask: Each serializer declares columns and types.

PD40.feature: Measure save/load time for large datasets.
PD40.likelyHackathonTask: Use timeout tests and timing output.
possibleApi: generateLargeDataset(int users, int posts, int messages)
